name: Restore accepted grouped_rows=8 base, then test 8->6 hot-band narrowing

summary.name: Restore accepted grouped_rows=8 base, then test 8->6 hot-band narrowing
summary.hypothesis: The accepted grouped_rows=8 + reversed row-pair + right-left PTX sweep + one-sync handoff + unroll 2 base is still the best locality shape, and the remaining question is whether a narrower kFixedHotBandPtxGroupedRows value of 6 preserves enough reuse without reintroducing the DRAM growth seen at grouped_rows=4.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Locality loss in the hot-band PTX consumer ordering and row reuse window, not the broader sweep or sync structure.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:right-left PTX sweep and one-sync handoff
summary.risk: If 6 is too narrow, it may recover less reuse than 8 while still missing the DRAM amplification seen with grouped_rows=4; however it is the cheapest remaining locality change on the accepted base.
metrics_to_recheck: warp stall mix around the handoff
summary.stop_condition: Stop if grouped_rows=6 does not improve over 25.934336 ms or if DRAM traffic rises toward the grouped_rows=4 regression.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
